title: Writable Layers, Volumes, Bind Mounts, tmpfs, Ownership, and Recovery · Changed-Environment Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer writable layers, volumes, bind mounts, tmpfs, ownership, and recovery into a new storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.
Create, inspect, mount, share, label, back up, restore, migrate, and remove named and anonymous volumes with explicit ownership.
Use bind mounts with deliberate host path, existence, read-only mode, propagation, SELinux, UID/GID, and portability boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write, keep Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Storage Backup Restore Test
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-storage-backup-restore-test-1p69qz". Tailor its storage-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Storage Backup Restore Test.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Storage Backup Restore Test into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Storage Backup Restore Test, keep Docker Storage Backup Restore Test connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-storage-backup-restore-test-1p69qz
  - competency: docker-storage-backup-restore-test
    artifact: storage-contract.yaml
    decision: storage backup restore test
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 1p69qz
    change: |
      mount-type: named-volume
      target: /var/lib/intake
      ownership: 10001:10001
      backup: application-consistent
      restore-test: fresh-volume
    verify: docker volume inspect intake-data && sha256sum restore-manifest.txt
    failure: Reject an anonymous volume as a named backup plan, bind-mount portability assumptions, or archive creation without a restore rehearsal.
    repair: Name ownership and lifecycle, quiesce or coordinate the application, checksum the artifact, and restore into a clean isolated volume.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Storage Backup Restore Test, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: A successful archive command proves the application can restore consistent data.
